case_definitions.name: relative time needs date helper
case_definitions.task: For the last 30 minutes of Zymtrace data, prepare the time range I should use for generated project metrics API calls.

case_definitions.name: unknown project id uses search
case_definitions.task: Find the Zymtrace project named llama-inference before we inspect its metrics.

case_definitions.name: cpu hot functions
case_definitions.task: Show the hottest CPU functions from 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 to 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: hot containers use entities
case_definitions.task: Which containers consumed the most CPU between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00?

case_definitions.name: first trace discovery is meta only
case_definitions.task: Find the hottest stack traces for python workers between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00, but do not dump full stacks yet.

case_definitions.name: high level flamegraph
case_definitions.task: Give me a CPU flamegraph for the vllm executable from 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 to 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: discover metrics before query
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, what metric names and attributes are active between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00?

case_definitions.name: known metric query
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, query the known request_latency histogram for service api between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: generated optimization advice
case_definitions.task: Show already-generated Zymtrace optimization recommendations for CPU traces from 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 to 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: raw events are explicit
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, fetch a small raw event sample between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00 for debugging the event payload shape.

case_definitions.name: discover common filter fields
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, show the most common available filter field values across profiling events between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project top pods endpoint
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, rank the hottest Kubernetes pods between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: default project metrics discovery skips search
case_definitions.task: For the default Zymtrace project, discover active GPU, CPU, model, service, token, and latency metric names between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: default project raw sample skips search
case_definitions.task: For the default Zymtrace project, fetch a small raw event sample between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00 to inspect payload shape.

case_definitions.name: cpu rank first containerized apps
case_definitions.task: Which containerized app should I optimize first by CPU cost from 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 to 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00?

case_definitions.name: gpu inference workflow starts with metrics
case_definitions.task: For the default Zymtrace project, start investigating why the vLLM inference workload has low GPU utilization between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00 by finding the relevant GPU, CPU, and framework metrics.

case_definitions.name: gpu call tree uses hot traces
case_definitions.task: Pull the GPU call tree for the vLLM workload from 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 to 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00, but only do first-pass discovery.

case_definitions.name: selected trace drilldown is bounded
case_definitions.task: Drill into hot trace prefix_hash 981237 and fetch exactly that full trace for the CPU profile from 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 to 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: full trace error recovers to discovery
case_definitions.task: The previous Zymtrace hot_traces call failed because meta_only=false was requested without a selected prefix_hash. Recover by doing first-pass CPU hot trace discovery for the default project between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: hot trace discovery is bounded
case_definitions.task: Show only the top 3 hot trace candidates as metadata for CPU between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00. Do not return full stacks yet.

case_definitions.name: resource fallback hot functions
case_definitions.task: In this tool-only Zymtrace matrix, choose the fallback call for the topfunctions MCP resource to rank hottest CPU functions between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project json flamegraph stays project scoped
case_definitions.task: For the default Zymtrace project, generate JSON flamegraph data for downstream processing between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project event aggregate counts
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, aggregate profiling event counts by kind between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: host metadata aggregation
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, aggregate host metadata between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00 to see OS, GPU, and architecture attributes.

case_definitions.name: project top architectures endpoint
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, rank the CPU architectures present in the profiling data between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project top clusters endpoint
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, rank the Kubernetes clusters contributing the most profiling activity between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project top containers endpoint
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, use the generated project stats endpoint to rank containers by profiling activity between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project top deployments endpoint
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, rank Kubernetes deployments by profiling activity between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project top hosts endpoint
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, rank hosts by profiling activity between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project top executables endpoint
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, rank main executable names by profiling activity between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project top namespaces endpoint
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, rank Kubernetes namespaces by profiling activity between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project top scripts endpoint
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, rank script names by profiling activity between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project top threads endpoint
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, rank thread names by profiling activity between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project top user tags endpoint
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, rank custom user tags by profiling activity between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

description: Compare stock and tuned descriptions for the Zymtrace MCP profiling-analysis tools.

choice.rationale: The user specified a relative time range ('last 30 minutes') and wants a time range for generated project metrics API calls, which require Unix milliseconds. The next step is to get the current local time and Unix milliseconds so the start and end timestamps can be computed.
The user asks to find a project by name before inspecting metrics. Per instructions, use projects_search when the user asks to find/search projects or names a non-default project without its UUID.

The user asks for the hottest CPU functions over an explicit RFC3339 time range, so the high-level topfunctions tool is the appropriate fallback for the topfunctions resource. No date-time resolution is needed.

The user asks for the containers with the highest CPU consumption over an explicit time range. The high-level topentities tool is the appropriate fallback for top container entities when resources cannot be read in this tool-only matrix.

results.case: first trace discovery is meta only

choice.rationale: The user asks for hottest stack traces, which maps to the high-level hot_traces tool. The time range is explicit, so get_date_time is not needed. The python worker scope can be applied via filter_text, and meta_only avoids dumping full stacks yet.

The user requested a CPU flamegraph for a specific executable and provided an explicit absolute time range, so get_date_time is not needed. The high-level flamegraph fallback has no executable filter parameter, so the project-scoped JSON flamegraph endpoint is the best single tool, using the default project_id because no other project was named.

The user asks for active metric names and attributes within an explicit time range for a specific project. The appropriate discovery tool is project_metrics_activity_aggr, which should be used before project_metrics_query for metric activity/attribute discovery. The range is absolute, so get_date_time is not needed; Unix milliseconds are supplied for the REST-style request body.

The user named a specific project UUID, a known histogram metric, a concrete service filter, and an explicit absolute time range, so the appropriate next tool is the project-scoped custom metrics query endpoint rather than discovery or date-time conversion.

The user explicitly asks to show already-generated Zymtrace optimization recommendations for CPU traces over a specified absolute time range, which matches the recommendations tool. No relative or omitted time range requires get_date_time.

The user requested a low-level raw event sample for a specific project and explicit time range to inspect payload shape, so the raw project events endpoint is the appropriate single tool. The explicit RFC3339 times are converted to Unix milliseconds for the REST-style project API, and a small limit is included for sampling.

The user asks for the most common available filter field values across profiling events for a specific project and explicit time window. The dedicated project-scoped aggregate tool for common filter-field values is the appropriate fallback; no get_date_time is needed because the range is absolute.

results.error: model did not return a JSON tool choice:

results.case: default project metrics discovery skips search

choice.rationale: The user asks to discover active metric names for GPU/CPU/inference-related dimensions in the default project over an explicit absolute time range. The guidance says to use project_metrics_activity_aggr before project_metrics_query, especially for GPU/inference metrics discovery. No get_date_time call is needed because the time range is provided explicitly; the request body uses Unix milliseconds for the project-scoped REST-style API.

results.case: default project raw sample skips search

choice.rationale: The user asks for a small raw event sample in the default project to inspect payload shape, which is a low-level raw event payload request. The time range is explicit, so get_date_time is not needed; Unix milliseconds are used for the project-scoped REST-style request.

The user asks for the containerized application to optimize first by CPU cost over an explicit time range. This is a high-level hot-entity profiling question, so the fallback tool for the topentities resource is appropriate; using entity=deployment best matches an application-level containerized workload.

results.case: gpu inference workflow starts with metrics

choice.rationale: The task asks to start by finding relevant GPU, CPU, and framework metrics for an explicit time window in the default project. project_metrics_activity_aggr is the preferred discovery step before project_metrics_query, especially for GPU/inference metric investigation. The explicit RFC3339 range is converted to Unix milliseconds, so get_date_time is not needed.

results.case: gpu call tree uses hot traces

choice.rationale: The user asks for a GPU call tree / stack-trace discovery for a vLLM workload over an explicit time range, and specifies first-pass discovery, which matches hot_traces with profile_type GPU, filter_text vLLM, and meta_only enabled.

The task asks to drill into a specific hot trace by prefix_hash for a CPU profile over an explicit RFC3339 time range, which maps directly to the hot_traces tool with the prefix_hash argument. No date/time lookup is needed because the range is explicit.

results.case: full trace error recovers to discovery

choice.rationale: The user explicitly requests first-pass CPU hot trace discovery after a failed detailed hot_traces call caused by meta_only=false without a prefix_hash. For first-pass discovery, hot_traces should be called with meta_only=true and no prefix_hash. The time range is explicit, so get_date_time is not needed.

The user asks for CPU hot trace candidates over an explicit time range and specifically wants metadata only, not full stacks. The high-level hot_traces tool is the appropriate fallback, with meta_only enabled. No get_date_time call is needed because the time range is explicit.
The task explicitly asks for the fallback tool corresponding to the topfunctions MCP resource to rank hottest CPU functions over a provided absolute time range, so no get_date_time call is needed.

results.case: project json flamegraph stays project scoped

choice.rationale: The user asks for JSON-format flamegraph data for downstream processing in the default project. This is project-scoped and specifically requests JSON flamegraph output, so project_flamegraph_json is the best match. The time range is explicit, so get_date_time is not needed; the default project UUID is used. CPU is selected as the assumed default profile type because no profile type was specified.

The user specified a project UUID and asked for a low-level aggregation of profiling event counts by kind over an explicit time range, so the project event aggregation endpoint is the appropriate single tool. The range is explicit, so get_date_time is not needed; Unix milliseconds are used for the generated project API request body.

The user specified a project UUID and explicitly asked to aggregate host metadata for OS, GPU, and architecture over a fixed time range, which matches projects_events_aggregate_host_metadata. No relative or omitted time range is present, so get_date_time is not needed.

The user provided a specific project UUID and explicit time range, and asks to rank CPU architectures present in profiling data. The project-scoped top-architectures aggregate tool directly matches this request; no date-time lookup or project search is needed.

The user provided an explicit project UUID and time range, and asked to rank Kubernetes clusters by profiling activity. The dedicated project-scoped top-clusters aggregation tool is the best match; get_date_time and projects_search are unnecessary because the time range and project UUID are explicit.

The user explicitly asked to use the generated project stats endpoint to rank containers by profiling activity for a specified project and absolute time range. The matching generated stats tool is projects_stats_aggregate_top_containers. The RFC3339 range was converted to Unix milliseconds, so get_date_time is not needed.

The user provided an explicit project UUID and explicit time range, and asks to rank Kubernetes deployments by profiling activity. The project-scoped top deployments stats aggregation endpoint is the most specific fallback tool for this request; no date-time lookup is needed because the range is absolute.

model did not return a JSON tool choice:

choice.rationale: The user specified a project UUID and asked to rank script names by profiling activity over an explicit absolute time range. The project-scoped top script names aggregation tool directly matches this request, and the generated project API should use Unix milliseconds for the time bounds.

The user specified a project UUID and asked to rank thread names by profiling activity over an explicit time range. The project-scoped top thread names aggregate tool directly matches this request, and the explicit RFC3339 range has been converted to Unix milliseconds for the REST-style request body.

The task is project-scoped with an explicit UUID and asks to rank custom user tags by profiling activity. The dedicated top-user-tags stats aggregation tool is the appropriate fallback; no project search or date-time lookup is needed because the project UUID and absolute time range are provided.

To prepare an accurate last-30-minutes time range for project metrics API calls, the current local date/time and Unix milliseconds are needed first; no profiling or metrics query should be generated until the current timestamp is known.
The project ID is not known yet, so the correct first step is to search projects by the requested project name before any metrics inspection.
The request asks for the hottest CPU functions over a specified time window, so the rank-first CPU workflow should start with topfunctions.

The task is a CPU-only rank-first question asking which containers consumed the most CPU in a specified time window, so the appropriate first step is to rank CPU entities by container using topentities. Any zymtrace-profiler entries should be ignored when interpreting the ranking.

results.case: first trace discovery is meta only

choice.rationale: The task asks for hottest stack traces, not a rendered flamegraph, and explicitly says not to dump full stacks yet. CPU is the default because this is a python-worker investigation with no GPU/accelerator terms. Per hot_traces rules, the first call should be discovery/meta-only with a limit no greater than 5.

The user explicitly asks for a CPU flamegraph, and the Zymtrace rules say to choose flamegraph when flamegraph data/rendering is explicitly requested. The provided flamegraph tool supports CPU and the requested time range; it does not expose an executable filter parameter for vllm.

The task asks to discover active metric names and attributes for a specific project and time window, which is exactly the purpose of the metrics activity aggregation endpoint. No profiling, ranking, flamegraph, or hot-trace workflow is requested.

The metric name and time range are known, and the task is to query metric values with a service filter, so project_metrics_query is the appropriate tool rather than metric discovery or profiling tools.
The user explicitly asks for already-generated Zymtrace optimization recommendations for CPU traces in a specified time range, which matches the recommendations tool with profile_type CPU.
The task asks for a small raw event sample to debug event payload shape, so the raw events fetch tool is the appropriate single tool. CPU/GPU profiling workflows do not apply.
The task asks for the most common available filter field values across profiling events for a specific project and time range, which directly matches projects_events_aggregate_top_field_values.

The task is a CPU-default entity ranking request for Kubernetes pods within a specific project and time window. The project-scoped top-pods aggregation is the most specific ranking tool, with CPU profile type and exclusion of zymtrace-profiler from ranking targets.

results.case: default project metrics discovery skips search

choice.rationale: The task asks to discover active metric names, and the Zymtrace GPU workflow requires starting with project_metrics_activity_aggr to find matching GPU/framework/CPU metrics before querying values or traces. No metric names are known yet, so a metric activity aggregation over the requested window is the correct first tool.

results.case: default project raw sample skips search

choice.rationale: The task explicitly asks to fetch a small raw event sample to inspect payload shape, so the raw events endpoint is the appropriate single tool. No CPU/GPU profiling workflow is needed.

This is a rank-first CPU-cost question asking which containerized app to optimize first, so the CPU workflow should begin by ranking top entities. Using entity=container identifies the hottest container target; zymtrace-profiler should be ignored when interpreting the ranking.

results.case: gpu inference workflow starts with metrics

choice.rationale: The request is about vLLM inference and low GPU utilization, so the GPU workflow applies. The first required step is to discover matching GPU, CPU, and framework metrics with project_metrics_activity_aggr before querying metric values or pulling hot traces.

results.case: gpu call tree uses hot traces

choice.rationale: The request is for a GPU call tree/hot-trace view for a vLLM workload, and it explicitly asks for first-pass discovery. Per the rules, call-tree/stack-trace analysis should use hot_traces rather than flamegraph unless a rendered flamegraph is requested, and the first hot_traces call must be discovery-only with meta_only=true. The vLLM filter scopes the discovery to the workload of interest.

The task asks to drill into a known CPU hot trace prefix_hash and fetch the full trace. Per the rules, full trace fetches use hot_traces with the selected prefix_hash and limit=1, not meta-only discovery.

results.case: full trace error recovers to discovery

choice.rationale: This is a recovery first-pass CPU hot trace discovery for the default project. Per Zymtrace rules, the initial hot_traces request must use meta_only=true and avoid requesting full traces without a selected prefix_hash; limit is set to 5.

The task explicitly asks for CPU hot trace candidates as metadata only, with no full stacks. Per the hot_traces workflow, the first call should use meta_only=true and a small limit, so hot_traces with limit 3 is the appropriate single tool.
Use the topfunctions fallback/resource-equivalent call to rank hottest CPU functions for the specified interval; this is a CPU rank-first request and no flamegraph or GPU workflow is requested.

results.case: project json flamegraph stays project scoped

choice.rationale: The user explicitly requested flamegraph data for the default Zymtrace project. Per the rules, explicit flamegraph-data requests should use flamegraph, and with no GPU/ALLOC indicators the profile type defaults to CPU.

The task asks for aggregated profiling event counts grouped by kind for a specified project and absolute time range, so the generic project event aggregation endpoint is the most appropriate single tool.

The task explicitly asks to aggregate host metadata for a known project and fixed time range, specifically OS, GPU, and architecture attributes, so the host metadata aggregation endpoint is the best single tool.

The task asks to rank CPU architectures present in profiling data for a specific project and fixed time window. The dedicated top-architectures aggregation endpoint is the most direct match, with CPU selected by default for this CPU-only/entity ranking request and zymtrace-profiler excluded from ranking targets.

The task asks to rank Kubernetes clusters by profiling activity for a specific project and time window. This directly matches the top-clusters project statistics tool. Because the request is entity-only and does not mention GPU/accelerators or allocations, it defaults to CPU profiling, with zymtrace-profiler excluded from ranking targets.

The task explicitly asks to use the generated project stats endpoint to rank containers by profiling activity for a given project and time range. Container ranking maps directly to projects_stats_aggregate_top_containers; entity-only/ranking investigations default to CPU, and zymtrace-profiler is excluded from ranking targets.

choice.rationale: The task is to rank main executable names by profiling activity for a specific project and time window. Entity-only profiling investigations default to CPU, and this project-specific top-main-executables aggregation is the most direct match while excluding zymtrace-profiler from ranking targets.

The task asks to rank Kubernetes namespaces by profiling activity for a specific project and time window. This is an entity-only ranking, which defaults to CPU, and the project-scoped top-namespaces aggregation is the direct tool. The request excludes zymtrace-profiler from ranking targets per the rules.

The task asks to rank script names by profiling activity for a specific project and time window. This is an entity-ranking request, so it defaults to CPU profiling. The project-specific top script names aggregation tool is the most direct match, with zymtrace-profiler excluded from ranking targets.

The task is an entity-only ranking request, so it defaults to CPU profiling. Since the project ID is provided and the requested entity is thread names, the project-specific top-thread-names aggregation is the best single tool. The request excludes zymtrace-profiler from ranking targets per the Zymtrace rules.

The task asks to rank custom user tags by profiling activity for a specific project and time window. This is an entity-ranking request with no GPU/ALLOC indication, so it defaults to CPU, and the project-specific top user tags aggregation is the most direct tool. zymtrace-profiler is excluded from ranking targets per rule.

The request uses a relative time range (“last 30 minutes”) and asks for a time range suitable for generated project metrics API calls, which require Unix milliseconds. The next step is to get the current time so the start_ts and end_ts can be computed accurately.

The user explicitly asks to find a named non-default Zymtrace project before using project-scoped metrics tools, so the next step is to search projects by name rather than using the default project_id.
The user asks for hottest CPU functions over an explicit RFC3339 time window, which is the primary use case for the topfunctions fallback tool in a tool-only environment.

The user asks for a ranked list of containers by CPU consumption over an explicit RFC3339 time window. topentities is the preferred high-level fallback tool for ranking hot runtime entities such as containers; no get_date_time is needed because the time range is absolute.

results.case: first trace discovery is meta only

choice.rationale: The user asks for hottest stack traces/call-tree discovery, and explicitly says not to dump full stacks yet, so hot_traces should be used with meta_only=true. The time range is already absolute RFC3339, so get_date_time is not needed; CPU is the default profile type.

The user explicitly requested a CPU flamegraph for a named executable over an absolute RFC3339 time range. In a resource-capable host the flamegraph resource would be read first, but in this tool-only matrix the fallback is the flamegraph tool.

The user asks to discover active metric names and attributes for a specific project over an absolute time window. project_metrics_activity_aggr is the metrics discovery tool; no project search is needed because the UUID is provided, and get_date_time is not needed because the timestamps are absolute.

The metric name is already known (request_latency), the user asks to query histogram metric values, and they provided an explicit project UUID plus absolute time range, so project_metrics_query is the appropriate next tool. Absolute RFC3339 times are converted to Unix milliseconds for the project API.

The user explicitly asks for already-generated optimization recommendations for CPU traces within an absolute RFC3339 time window, which matches the recommendations tool and does not require get_date_time.

The user explicitly asks for a small raw event sample for a specified project and provides an absolute time range, so project_events_raw is the appropriate tool. The RFC3339 times are converted to Unix milliseconds, and no get_date_time call is needed.

The request is for the most common available filter field values across profiling events in a specific project and absolute time window, which matches projects_events_aggregate_top_field_values. No get_date_time call is needed because the range is already absolute; timestamps are supplied as Unix milliseconds for the project API.

The user provided a specific project UUID and asked to rank the hottest Kubernetes pods over an absolute time window. The dedicated project-scoped top pods endpoint is the best match; CPU is the default profile type because GPU or allocation data was not requested. Absolute timestamps are converted to Unix milliseconds for the project API request body.

results.case: default project metrics discovery skips search

choice.rationale: The user asks to discover active metric names and useful dimensions for GPU, CPU, model, service, token, and latency in the default project over an absolute time window, so the metrics discovery/activity aggregation tool is the appropriate next call. Absolute timestamps were provided, so get_date_time is not needed; the default project UUID is used.

results.case: default project raw sample skips search

choice.rationale: The task explicitly asks for a small raw event sample for the default project over an absolute time range, so the raw project event endpoint is the appropriate single tool. The RFC3339 timestamps were converted to Unix milliseconds required by the project API, and a small approx_limit is included to inspect payload shape.

The user asks which containerized app to optimize first by CPU cost in an explicit RFC3339 time window. topentities is the high-level fallback tool for ranking hot runtime entities, and containerized apps should use entity=container. No get_date_time is needed because the time range is absolute.

results.case: gpu inference workflow starts with metrics

choice.rationale: The task is a metrics-first GPU/inference investigation for the default project and asks to find relevant GPU, CPU, and framework metrics. The appropriate next tool is project_metrics_activity_aggr, which is intended for discovering active metric names and dimensions before querying values. The user provided an absolute time range, so get_date_time is not needed; the RFC3339 timestamps are represented as Unix milliseconds in the project API request.

results.case: gpu call tree uses hot traces

choice.rationale: The user asks for a GPU call tree and specifies first-pass discovery, so hot_traces is the appropriate high-level tool with meta_only=true and a small limit. The time range is already absolute RFC3339, so get_date_time is not needed.

The user explicitly asks to drill into a specific hot trace prefix_hash and fetch exactly that full CPU trace for an absolute RFC3339 time window, so hot_traces should be called with meta_only=false, the provided prefix_hash, and limit=1.

results.case: full trace error recovers to discovery

choice.rationale: The task asks to recover from a failed full hot_traces call by performing first-pass CPU hot trace discovery over an absolute RFC3339 time window. Per tool guidance, the first discovery call should use meta_only=true with a small limit and no prefix_hash.
